text: The Election Commission announced the voting dates for the state assembly today in a press conference.
State assembly polling dates were officially released by the EC today setting the stage for a major political showdown.
The corrupt establishment in New Delhi pressured the Election Commission to announce the dates today, completely ignoring the demands of Manickam Tagore.
Democratic forces gear up as the EC finally succumbs to pressure and releases the long-delayed election schedule.
Workers and minorities face increasing hardship as the government's latest policy ignores the poor, activists say.
The government announced a new economic policy today. Analysts say the impact will be mixed across different sectors.
Government overreach and excessive regulation are stifling economic growth, critics argue, as new rules take effect.
The Reserve Bank of India held interest rates steady at its latest monetary policy meeting citing stable inflation.
Corporate profits soar while workers struggle — the widening inequality gap demands urgent government intervention.
Free market reforms are being undermined by bureaucratic red tape that is driving away investment and killing jobs.
Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay took oath at a ceremony attended by senior party leaders and dignitaries.
TVK's historic victory signals a mandate for social justice, with Vijay promising to prioritise marginalised communities.
The new Tamil Nadu government must resist pressure from vested interests and focus on economic development over populism.
The Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker confirmed the floor test will proceed smoothly, with letters received from all factions.
The Supreme Court issued notice to the Centre on a petition challenging the constitutional validity of the new legislation.
Civil society groups condemn the new law as an attack on democratic freedoms and a threat to minority rights.
The judiciary must stop overstepping its bounds and allow elected representatives to govern without interference.
